title: Boats and Rock Pools: Making a Yacht Club a Home for Sea Creatures
summary: A yacht club in Northern Ireland has added 10 artificial rock pools to its seawall. These pools are like little homes for sea creatures. They are made of concrete and look like honeycombs. The pools are attached to the wall with bolts. They were inspired by similar pools in the Isle of Wight. The project is called 'Greening the Grey' and aims to help nature by adding these pools to man-made walls. The pools will hold water and provide a safe place for animals like crabs, starfish, and sea snails. This is important because climate change is making the sea warmer and harder for some animals to live. The people behind the project hope to see a lot of different sea life in the pools. They also hope to see some unexpected creatures. The rock pools are a new idea in Northern Ireland and could help increase the number of animals living along the coast.

questions.question: What is one problem that makes it hard for sea creatures to live?

background_read: Rock pools are natural holes in rocks near the sea that fill with water when the tide comes in. They are home to many small sea creatures like crabs, starfish, and sea snails. Sometimes people make artificial rock pools to help nature in places where there are no natural rock pools. These artificial pools can be attached to walls or other man-made structures along the coast. They give sea creatures a place to live and help increase the number of different animals in the area.
Article_Structure: WHO: A yacht club in Northern Ireland, with help from Mourne Gullion Strangford Geopark, installed the rock pools.
WHAT: 10 artificial rock pools were added to the seawall to provide homes for sea creatures.
WHERE: Strangford Yacht Club in Whiterock Bay, Northern Ireland.
WHY: To help marine life by giving them a safe place to live, especially because of climate change.

perspectives.description: The project helps increase biodiversity and provides a refuge for marine life affected by climate change.
The yacht club is becoming a place that not only hosts boats but also supports nature.
